channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: DRC Diplomatic Moves Undermine Rwanda's Influence in Regional Affairs
item.description: The article discusses the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) minister for regional integration, Floribert Anzuluni, who recently traveled to Brussels and Paris as part of a strategic diplomatic initia...

item.title: AfCFTA Chief Calls for Faster Trade Integration to Strengthen Africa’s Single Market
item.description: Wamkele Mene, the Secretary-General of the African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA), amesema kuwa Afrika haiwezi kufikia maendeleo ya kweli ya kiuchumi ikiwa itaendelea kugawanyika katika masoko y...

item.title: Ukraine Strikes Russian Retail Giant's Warehouses and Romania's Airspace as War Escalates
item.description: Ukraine has launched strikes against Russian online retail giant Wildberries' warehouses in St Petersburg, targeting infrastructure supporting the Russian military. The attacks mark the fifth such inc...

item.title: TikToker Shares Construction Progress of Tony Ndiema's House Ahead of Burial
item.description: A TikToker shared a walking tour of Tony Ndiema's rural homestead, showcasing the construction progress on his house as preparations for his burial begin. The video captures a partially built brick ho...

item.title: Community Action Saves Woman's Vehicle from Oncoming Train in Nairobi
item.description: A woman's car stalled on railway tracks in Embakasi, Nairobi, with a train rapidly approaching. Bystanders, including a man who left his own car, rushed to push the stranded vehicle off the tracks. Th...

item.title: House Democrat calls for Barron Trump to testify on Andrew and Tristan Tate extradition
item.description: A House Democrat has called for Barron Trump, son of former President Donald Trump, to testify regarding allegations of political interference in the extradition case of Andrew and Tristan Tate. The r...

item.title: Africa Corps navigates sanctions by rerouting military cargo to Mali
item.description: A Russian military convoy has successfully bypassed sanctions by taking a risky new route through Burkina Faso to deliver arms to Mali. The shipment, which circumvents Guinea's refusal to accept a san...

item.title: Terrorist Attack in KP’s Tank District Claims 15 Lives, Including 14 Security Personnel
item.description: A terrorist attack occurred at a joint police checkpost in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa's Tank district, resulting in the martyrdom of 15 individuals, including 14 security personnel. The military's media wing,...

item.title: Tanzania upgrades Pande Reserve for Afcon 2027 preparations
item.description: The Pande Reserve in Tanzania has undergone significant upgrades to prepare for the upcoming Africa Cup of Nations (Afcon) 2027 tournament. This development marks a major step in enhancing the country...

item.title: Canadian financial sector stock ideas highlighted in market analysis
item.description: Scott Barlow, The Globe and Mail's market strategist, outlines key stock recommendations for Canadian diversified financials, emphasizing resilient growth and value opportunities. Analyst Phil Hardie ...

item.title: Juergen Klopp appointed Germany national team coach following World Cup exit
item.description: The German football federation has appointed Juergen Klopp as the new head coach of the national team, succeeding Julian Nagelsmann who resigned after Germany failed to advance past the last 16 of the...

item.title: Government opens state-of-the-art pediatric hospital in Clark for free healthcare access
item.description: President Ferdinand 'Bongbong' Marcos Jr. announced the opening of the Jose B. Lingad Children's Medical Center in Clark Freeport, Pampanga, set to serve patients starting August 1, 2026. This facilit...

item.title: Nigerians' Guide to Joining U.S. Army in 2026: Requirements and Steps
item.description: The article explains that Nigerians cannot join the U.S. Army directly from Nigeria or on a tourist/student visa. They must have a valid U.S. Green Card or citizenship. Key requirements include age li...

item.title: Gauteng Taxi Strike Averted Through Negotiations
item.description: The planned Gauteng taxi shutdown was called off after crisis talks between the provincial government and taxi industry leaders, reports EWN. The Gauteng Department of Transport, SANTACO, and the Nati...

item.title: Demographic Breakdown of Andy Burnham's Cabinet Reveals Gender Parity and Regional Representation
item.description: Andy Burnham's cabinet marks a historic shift in British politics, with women comprising half the team for the first time. This represents a significant departure from past decades, where female repre...

item.title: Supreme Court to hear plea over distance of alternate Friday namaz site near Bhojshala on July 29
item.description: The Supreme Court has agreed to hear on July 29 a plea filed by the Muslim side regarding the implementation of its interim directions in the long-running Bhojshala dispute in Dhar, Madhya Pradesh. Du...

item.title: KPMG suspends partner over confidential document mishandling
item.description: KPMG has suspended its former chief operating officer Eileen Hoggett from the firm's partnership following an internal investigation. The decision comes after the firm discovered she had stored printe...

item.title: Study Reveals Canada's Cost of Happiness at $155,850
item.description: A study conducted by digital financial services provider Remitly, based on research from Purdue University, calculates the cost of happiness in different countries. Adjusted for local purchasing power...

item.title: PM Shehbaz Addresses Terrorism and Regional Security at Balochistan Workshop
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif addressed the Balochistan National Workshop, emphasizing India's direct involvement in terrorism within Pakistan. He accused India of sponsoring terrorist activities and ...

item.title: Alleged Kingpin Vusimusi 'Cat' Matlala Faces Attempted Murder Charges in Johannesburg High Court
item.description: The attempted murder trial of Vusimusi 'Cat' Matlala, a prominent taxi boss, media personality, and musician, continues in the Johannesburg high court. Matlala and co-accused are facing charges relate...
